Definition.text: Use is a keyword and refers to an in-game action known as skill use.

Purpose.text: Describe a unit of time and action for a skill’s execution flow before effects resolve.

Core Rules.text: Skills can have stored uses and other use-related characteristics. Use generally includes animated cast-time and additional time-counted parts.

Internal Rules.text: When a skill has both cast time and cooldown in its use flow, those phases are part of one use timing path.

Calculation Order.text: Use timing is evaluated as cast-time action plus subsequent timed parts such as cooldown; this total defines use frequency.

Priority Rules.text: Specific per-skill use modifiers (for example stored uses, cooldown prevention, or cooldown bypass rules) override generic use timing assumptions.

Preconditions.text: An action must be in a skill-use path that exposes cast time and/or cooldown behavior.

Postconditions.text: The skill’s usable frequency is fixed by resolved use timing and gating terms.

Limitations.text: Use is descriptive of timing flow and does not itself define accuracy, evade, resist, or hit math.

Caps.text: No universal cap is defined for Use; caps are source-specific (e.g. stored uses or cooldown restrictions).

Formula.text: frequency = cast_time + cooldown (where both parts are present and applied to the use action).

Conflicts.text: Use timing is distinct from Attack/Spell categorization and cannot replace those tags.

Exceptions.text: Some skills have no explicit cooldown but still have use-related timing via cast time or other time-counted phases.

Hidden Rules.text: Use is presented as a keyword-level construct that can be attached to skill metadata and not only to player-facing button presses.

Developer Notes.text: Record Use as a timing construct and link it to Cast Time and cooldown in schema relationships.

Historical Changes.text: Source snapshot uses an older revision date; no explicit cross-patch deltas captured here.

text: If skill uses cast time and cooldown, use frequency is cast_time + cooldown.
A stored-use skill is still a Use-capable skill with special timing constraints.
Use is not the same as Cast.
Use is not a damage formula term; it is a use-timing construct.
Zero cooldown does not mean Use is absent.

cannot_affect: spells that explicitly bypass use timing
